subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.1.1.1: Demonstrate use of appropriate language orally in specific situations
indicator_description: Use appropriate register in everyday communication (informal and formal) with diverse partners on grade-level topics/texts/issues
examplars:
Identify formal situations. E.g. interactions between strangers on varied themes, announcements, standards for work, school and public offices and business settings.
Use appropriate language to participate in formal interactions. (No slang/jargon).
Identify informal conversations: E.g. casual or intimate relationship between friends and acquaintances, family and teammates, different situations in the classroom etc.
Use appropriate language to participate in conversations in the following situations: Greetings, requests, encouragements, partings etc. NB: Examples of informal language include; slang words, jargon, contracted forms and non-verbal communication.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.1.1.1: Demonstrate use of appropriate language orally in specific situations
indicator_description: Ask questions that elicit elaboration and respond to others' questions in conversation
examplars:
Identify words that can help give elaborate responses to questions in conversation (e.g. why, how, for what reason).
Engage in conversation using these words to elicit elaboration
E.g.Ama: Do you think girls should study courses in the male dominated areas?
Aziz: Yes
Ama: Why do you think so?
NB: Includes gender, equity, equality, and inclusion issues.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.1.1.1: Demonstrate use of appropriate language orally in specific situations
indicator_description: Use appropriate language orally to describe experiences about oneself and others
examplars:
Use language: tense structures, variety of sentences, figurative expressions etc.
vocabulary: nouns, adjectives, adverbs, etc. to communicate about one's experiences and those of others in specific situations e.g. the hospital, shopping in a market/supermarket, at a bus terminal/station, etc.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.1.1.1: Demonstrate use of appropriate language orally in specific situations
indicator_description: Listen to and give accurate directions to familiar places
examplars:
Use appropriate language (e.g. opposite, adjacent, a few metres away, ten minute-walk/drive, turn left/right) and landmarks (church, mosque, hospital, filling station, etc.) to give directions.
Direct different individuals using a range of these vocabularies and expressions appropriately to given locations.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.1.1.1: Demonstrate use of appropriate language orally in specific situations
indicator_description: Use techniques (voice modulation and eye contact) for effective oral communication
examplars:
Converse using appropriate voice (pace, volume, tone, stress) e.g. word and sentence stress. E.g.: increase (noun) and increase (verb).
Maintain eye contact (look in the eyes/face of the person speaking, maintain appropriate posture and facial expression) in conversation.
Engage in conversation using voice modulation and maintaining eye contact. Monitor how your partner shows this in the conversation.
Demonstrate appropriate non-interruptive verbal and non-verbal cues/responses to show attention.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.1.2.1: Demonstrate the ability to listen to extended reading and identify key information
indicator_description: Listen to level-appropriate text attentively and identify key information

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.1.2.1: Demonstrate the ability to listen to extended reading and identify key information
indicator_description: Listen to, discuss ideas and share opinions from a level-appropriate text
examplars:
Listen to and write key points from texts/speeches/presentations.
Discuss key points identified and share opinions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.1.3.1: Articulate English speech sounds to develop confidence and skills in listening and speaking
indicator_description: Produce pure vowel sounds (short vowels) in context
examplars:
Identify and produce pure vowels e.g. /a/, /i/, /e/ in context.
Articulate pure vowels clearly in speech.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.1.3.1: Articulate English speech sounds to develop confidence and skills in listening and speaking
indicator_description: Produce pure vowel sounds (long vowels) in context
examplars:
Identify and produce long vowels in context. E.g.: part, peel, bloom.
Distinguish between long and short vowels in context.
Listen to and produce sounds as used in connected speech.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.1.3.1: Articulate English speech sounds to develop confidence and skills in listening and speaking
indicator_description: Produce diphthongs in context (centring and closing)
examplars:
Identify centring diphthongs e.g. /ea//ia/ in context.
Identify closing diphthongs e.g. /au/, /ai/ in context.
Listen and distinguish between vowels and diphthongs in context.
Use vowels and diphthongs accurately in connected speech.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.2.1.1: Demonstrate increasing confidence and enjoyment in independent reading
indicator_description: Read and understand a range of texts using monitoring and mental visualisation strategies to interpret texts
examplars:
Engage narrative texts and create mental pictures to aid understanding.
Read fluently to build confidence.
Engage in meaningful interaction with text and peers.
Monitor for understanding and self-correct where necessary.
Make connections with what is read to own experiences.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.2.1.1: Demonstrate increasing confidence and enjoyment in independent reading
indicator_description: Use prediction to assess and improve understanding of texts

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.2.1.1: Demonstrate increasing confidence and enjoyment in independent reading
indicator_description: Generate and answer questions to increase understanding and independent reading of fiction texts
examplars:
Read a text closely for implicit meaning.
Write questions to guide reading.
Look back at questions as you read and create a link with text.
Provide and interpret evidence to support understanding.
Answer questions accurately.
Use the answers to identify the main idea of the text.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.2.1.1: Demonstrate increasing confidence and enjoyment in independent reading
indicator_description: Use text structure to understand and read texts independently
examplars:
Interact with a variety of texts.
Examine the topic sentences that give clues to a specific structure. Identify how the structure (how the information is organised) of the text influences meaning. These may include:
description,
sequence,
problem and solution,
cause and effect and
compare and contrast.
Use a graphic organiser to make a chart of the text structure.
Write paragraphs that follow a specific text structure.
Read independently and identify how text structure helps with understanding other texts.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.2.1.2: Read, comprehend and interpret texts
indicator_description: Identify the main text features of a non-literary texts
examplars:
Use samples of varied texts such as non-fiction articles, formal letters (business letters, newsletters), etc. to identify text features (Print, Graphic, and Organisational):
Print Features: title, heading/heading, bold Print, italics caption, etc.
Graphic Features: Photograph with or without caption, Drawing, Diagram, Map, Graph, Chart, etc.
Organisational Features: Table of Content, Index, Glossary, etc.
Discuss how the text features aid in the comprehension of a text. E.g. A title indicates the topic, subject matter or the main idea of an entire text.
Bold print signals important vocabulary and/or a phrase that is integral to understanding the content of a text.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.2.1.2: Read, comprehend and interpret texts
indicator_description: Interpret non-fiction texts pointing out attitudes, opinions, biases and facts
examplars:
Write out essential questions.
Read the text.
Make connections between texts and personal experiences.
Research or make inferences and share opinions on biases and facts in texts.
Distinguish between facts and opinions in a range of non-fiction texts.
Interpret the different attitudes exhibited by the choice of words in texts to support understanding.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.2.1.2: Read, comprehend and interpret texts
indicator_description: Interpret a non-literary text showing personal and supporting responses with textual evidences
examplars:
Read a variety of texts discussing the main ideas.
Identify how a writer's choice of words and intentions contribute to meaning.
Differentiate viewpoints with textual evidence.
Examine the impact of different viewpoints and how these contribute to meaning.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.2.2.1: Demonstrate an understanding in summarising
indicator_description: Use summarising to understand key ideas in a range of texts
examplars:
Read a variety of passages and identify the main ideas. Analyse and decide what is important.
Do not write the same words as the author.
Think and write in your own words.
Ask, "What is the whole write up about?"
Restate or map out the gist/main idea and key details.
Put up a defence for the choice/ answer.
Restate the main ideas in own words.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.2.2.2: Demonstrate understanding of textual evidence that supports a writing piece
indicator_description: Determine and analyse central and supporting ideas of texts
examplars:
Read a variety of texts for main ideas.
Identify the main idea/topic sentence in the paragraphs.
Identify supporting details and how these relate to main details in texts.
Summarise main ideas of texts in own words and peer edit it.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.1: Apply the knowledge of word classes and their functions in Communication.
indicator_description: Demonstrate command and application of nouns in speaking and texts
examplars:
Identify noun types (common, proper, countable and uncountable, concrete) from passages.
Construct sentences using noun types.
Categorise plural noun forms (emphasis on irregular nouns) in passages e.g. mouse - mice, child - children, sheep - sheep, half-halves.
Use plural noun forms in paragraphs.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.1: Apply the knowledge of word classes and their functions in Communication.
indicator_description: Use types of pronouns accurately in speaking and texts
examplars:
Explore the use of types of pronouns in narratives:
Personal Pronouns
Possessive Pronouns
Relative Pronouns
Reflexive Pronouns
Interrogative Pronouns
Demonstrative Pronouns
Use pronouns in contextual sentences.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.1: Apply the knowledge of word classes and their functions in Communication.
indicator_description: Explore accurate use of adjectives in texts
examplars:
Recognise the effective use of adjectives in paragraphs:
directly before nouns. E.g. Asiedu is an excellent singer.
with the verb "to be" to describe the subject of the sentence. E.g. Adzo was very tired.
Use adjectives with sense verbs (sight, taste, smell, touch, hearing) or verbs of appearance to modify the nouns, which come before the verb. E.g. The fish tasted awful. He seemed very upset.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.1: Apply the knowledge of word classes and their functions in Communication.
indicator_description: Relate forms of verbs to everyday activities (Tense & Aspects) in context, role play, radio, TV, films and narratives
examplars:
Present Tense
1. Identify the use of verbs that show present tense and aspect in sentences
simple present tense - I wash a car.
present progressive - I am washing a car.
present perfect - I have washed the car.
present perfect progressive - I have been washing the car
Past Tense
2. Identify the use of verbs that show past tense and aspect in sentences:
simple past tense - I washed the car.
past progressive - I was washing the car.
past perfect - I had washed the car.
past perfect progressive - I had been washing the car.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.1: Apply the knowledge of word classes and their functions in Communication.
indicator_description: Use adverbs to modify verbs accurately at the phrase and sentence level
examplars:
Recognise the effective use of adverbs in paragraphs;
to give more information about the verb. E.g. She slowly entered the room.
to give more information about the adjective. E.g. The test was extremely difficult.
Explore using the first adverb to give more information about the second adverb in sentences. E.g. The cheetah runs incredibly quickly.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.1: Apply the knowledge of word classes and their functions in Communication.
indicator_description: Use conjunctions accurately to link ideas in everyday discourse
examplars:
Explore sentences using coordinating conjunctions. E.g. for, and, nor, but, or, yet, and so.
Construct sentences using correlative conjunctions. E.g. either... or, neither... nor, and not only... but also.
Use subordinating conjunctions to link ideas. E.g. because, since, as, although, though, while, and whereas.
Use adverbs that function as conjunctions in sentences. E.g. until, after, or before.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.1: Apply the knowledge of word classes and their functions in Communication.
indicator_description: Demonstrate command of the use of prepositions in daily discourse (TV, radio, social media, news, home, role play)
examplars:
Use complex prepositions (more than one word) in constructing sentences. e.g. consist of, in front of, on behalf of, in view of, in spite of, due to, near to, because of, by means of, by dint of (hard work), etc.
E.g.:
The tree is in front of the house.
The prefect spoke on behalf of the class.
Use complex prepositions to express:
place e.g. near/close to, in front of, out of, far from, by the side of, etc.
time e.g. in time of, about to, during the course of, at noon, for one week, etc.
reason e.g. due to, because of, on account of, as a result of, etc.
concession e.g. in spite of, apart from etc.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.1: Apply the knowledge of word classes and their functions in Communication.
indicator_description: Identify and use determiners in speaking and texts
examplars:
Identify determiners from texts.
Articles are the most popular types of determiners. E.g. definite article 'the,' indefinite 'a,' and 'an.'
Possessives provide context in a sentence by informing the reader what belongs to the subject, such as 'our,' 'your,' my,' 'their,' 'her,' and 'his'. E.g. Her car is over there.
Demonstratives (that, this, there, these, and those) identify an object based on its location.
Use determiners in sentences and paragraphs.
E.g. That cat is very fat. An apple fell from the tree. Is your coffee on the counter?

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.2: Demonstrate command of structural and functional use of sentences
indicator_description: Identify and use subject and predicate in text
examplars:
Distinguish elements of a sentence.
Use the knowledge of the composition of subject.
Know the composition of predicate of a sentence.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.3: Demonstrate command of structural and functional use of sentences.
indicator_description: Demonstrate command and use of compound sentences
examplars:
Construct independent clauses.
E.g. Kofi arrived late. He missed the exam.
Join two or more independent clauses to form compound sentences using coordinating conjunctions (and, but, or, so, etc.)
Analyse compound sentences to identify the various independent clauses in them.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.4: Apply knowledge of clauses in communication
indicator_description: Use dependent and independent clauses appropriately in speaking and writing
examplars:
Recognise sentences as independent/dependent clauses in texts.
E.g. We danced at the party all night long.
Construct sentences by combining dependent and independent clauses.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.5: Demonstrate understanding of the use of conditional tenses in communication
indicator_description: Use conditional sentences in communication to indicate a possible condition and its possible result
examplars:
Construct simple sentences using the future time, e.g. We will go home tomorrow.
Construct complex sentences and analyse them to show the main clause and the subordinate clause(s). e.g. When we came, they were at a meeting. When we came subordinate clause. They were at a meeting - main clause
Use if- clauses appropriately in communication (to indicate varied possible conditions and their possible results). e.g. If it rains, we will get there late.
Analyse given conditional sentences (main clause and subordinate clauses).
Note: In a Type I conditional sentence, the tense in the 'if' clause is the simple present, and the tense in the main clause is the simple future. E.g. I will be happy if you come to my party. The 'If clause' is the condition while main clause is the result and the order of the clauses are not fixed. Conditional sentence Type I is used to communicate real situations.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.6: Demonstrate mastery of use of active and passive voice
indicator_description: Use passive sentences for a range of functions
examplars:
Use the passive voice to:
describe processes.
talk/write about past actions (when we do not know who did them).
talk/write about past actions (when we know who did them).
write reports and other formal texts.
Construct sentences in the active voice (subject, verb and object). E.g. The class won the game.
Reconstruct active sentences into the passive voice. E.g. The game was won by the class.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.6: Demonstrate mastery of use of active and passive voice
indicator_description: Demonstrate use and command of reported speech
examplars:
Compose sentences in the direct speech and change them to reported speech.
Report what people say in formal and informal situations.
e.g. 'She agreed she would meet me after school.' 'He regretted he was busy on Saturday and wouldn't be able to come'. 'You admitted there wasn't any homework.' 'He reported he had been walking along the road when the car hit the tree.'
Compose news reports.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.7: Show understanding and use of question tags in communication
indicator_description: Use question tags accurately
examplars:
Listen to dialogues involving question tags.
Identify statements with question tags. e.g. 'You're not going to play football today, are you?' 'You like tea, don't you?' 'She's older than me, isn't she?'
Compose statements with question tags.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.2.1: Demonstrate use and mastery of capitalisation and punctuation in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use punctuation marks (question, exclamation, full-stop, comma) in given texts
examplars:
Identify and use punctuation marks (question, exclamation, full-stop, comma) in given texts.
Correct punctuation errors in paragraphs.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.3.1: Demonstrate appropriate use of vocabulary in communication
indicator_description: Apply vocabulary appropriately in specific contexts
examplars:
Use appropriate vocabulary in specific contexts and situations. E.g.:
1. When asking for and giving advice...
2. When agreeing and disagreeing...
3. when ordering food in a restaurant
4. when speaking at a friend's birthday party
5. when in conversation with peers, adults etc.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.3.4.1: Demonstrate understanding of use of aesthetic language to enrich communication
indicator_description: Explore the use of proverbs to enrich communication
examplars:
Identify and examine proverbs in context to figure out their meanings.
Find Ghanaian proverbs to match the English ones identified.
Use proverbs to construct meaningful sentences.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.1.1: Develop, organise and express ideas coherently and cohesively in writing
indicator_description: Use cohesive devices (pronouns, punctuations and conjunctions) to link sentences
examplars:
Consolidate basic understanding of compound sentences using conjunctions (connectives): nor, or, so, then, yet.
Write complex sentences using subordinating conjunctions:
Use pronouns to connect ideas in paragraphs:
Use appropriate punctuation...
NB. Use logical connectors, pronouns and the repetition of words, synonyms and antonyms to create a cohesive text.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.1.2: Create different paragraphs on a given topic
indicator_description: Organise information in a logical manner.
examplars:
Write a paragraph beginning with topic (main) sentence and supporting sentences.
Organise sentences in a logical sequence to create a coherent paragraph that is appropriate for the text type...
Use logical connectors to link sentences in a paragraph: ...
NB. Use logical connectors, pronouns and the repetition of words, synonyms and antonyms to create a cohesive text.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.2.1: Develop, organise and express ideas coherently and cohesively in writing for a variety of purposes (description, narration, exposition/information and persuasion), audiences and contexts
indicator_description: Write personal narratives using effective techniques incorporating descriptive details and logical event sequences.
examplars:
Plan and record ideas detailing personal experiences or past events: Use: descriptive details (setting, experiences or series of events). first-person point of view to convey feelings, experiences and thoughts. appropriate grammatical structures. adjectives and adjective phrases for producing vivid descriptions. adverbs and adverbials for describing how an action has been carried out. connectors for showing sequence of events. direct and indirect speech for variation and adding interest. nouns and noun phrases (e.g., participants, objects). prepositions and prepositional phrases. first-person pronouns.
Edit/Proofread the writing for sense, meaning and effect (targeted audience reaction).
Publish writing using different media including ICT.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.2.1: Develop, organise and express ideas coherently and cohesively in writing for a variety of purposes (description, narration, exposition/information and persuasion), audiences and contexts
indicator_description: Use precise (technical) vocabulary, phrases and sensory language to convey a vivid mental picture of people and experiences
examplars:
Describe people using precise words (e.g. He staggers, to say precisely how someone walks), phrases and sensory details (words that appeal to sight, sound, smell, taste, and touch).
Use appropriate grammatical structures: ...
Revise and edit the writing for sense or meaning, and effect (emotional reaction).
Proofread to self-correct or peer-correct.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.2.1: Develop, organise and express ideas coherently and cohesively in writing for a variety of purposes (description, narration, exposition/information and persuasion), audiences and contexts
indicator_description: Create advertisements to persuade a given audience to take decisions on products and services
examplars:
Compose advertisement for the sale of products, services, events following appropriate format/style using process approach: e.g. Title, body information about the product or service...
Use: the simple present... modals or imperative... mental verbs... verb phrases... rhetorical questions and repetition for persuasion
Edit/Proofread the writing for sense or meaning, and effect (emotional reaction).
Publish writing using different media including ICT
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.2.1: Develop, organise and express ideas coherently and cohesively in writing for a variety of purposes (description, narration, exposition/information and persuasion), audiences and contexts
indicator_description: Compose a paragraph to explain a process, social and natural phenomena (how to do or use something, how something works)
examplars:
Write the steps taken to do or make something or how something works (e.g. rules of a game or sport, recipes, instructions on how to use a device, volcanic eruption, flooding) by: writing the aim of the text... listing the materials needed... sequencing and/or describing items to be carried out... using appropriate text features...
Use: adjectives and adjective phrases... connectors... nouns and noun phrases... quantifiers... simple present...
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.2.2:Apply writing skills to specific life situations
indicator_description: Compose informal letters on varied topics using appropriate format
examplars:
Identify features of informal letters (writer's address, date, salutation, body, subscription, name).
Use the correct features of informal letters appropriately.
Write informal letters on a range of themes to friends and relatives. (E.g. of themes; school life, excursions, games, festivals etc.).
Correct errors of informal letters written by others.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.2.2:Apply writing skills to specific life situations
indicator_description: Compose formal writing (application, invitation, email, media texts) on given topics using appropriate format
examplars:
Identify features of formal letters...
Write formal letters to offices...
Correct errors of formal letters written by others.
write notices to class or club members to provide updates on rules, schedules or programmes.
Create a variety of media texts for different purposes and audiences using appropriate forms, conventions, and techniques.
Reflect on and identify strengths, areas for improvement, and the strategies found most helpful in understanding and creating media texts.
Edit/Proofread the writing for sense or meaning, and effect (emotional reaction).
Publish writing using different media including ICT.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.2.2:Apply writing skills to specific life situations
indicator_description: Take notes for academic and other purposes
examplars:
Identify and record: source information... headings... key points... triggers... further reading...
Identify and select key ideas.
Organise ideas and make connections.
Plan and structure written assignments.
Write notes while listening to the teacher, reading a text, viewing a video recording or revising.
Edit/Proofread the writing for sense or meaning, and effect (emotional reaction).
Record/represent writing in a flow chart, illustrations and notes in other media including ICT.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.2.2:Apply writing skills to specific life situations
indicator_description: Design notices and posters for different purposes and audiences
examplars:
Write notices/posters of different lengths for different purposes and audiences, using appropriate forms, conventions, and techniques (posters on a school excursion, notices on voluntary cleaning etc.)
Edit/Proofread the writing for sense or meaning, and effect (emotional reaction).
Record/represent writing in a flow chart and illustrations and other design packages including ICT.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.2.2:Apply writing skills to specific life situations
indicator_description: Write articles on given issues for publication in class and club magazines.
examplars:
Identify the topic for, purpose of, and audience for article writing.
Generate ideas about more challenging topics and identify those most appropriate for the purpose.
Write, using a variety of strategies and a wide range of print and electronic resources.
Sort and classify ideas and information for writing a variety of articles.
Write articles of different lengths on given issues for publication(e.g., a rap poem or jingle, to express a personal view to the class; a report for a community newspaper about a public meeting on an environmental issue affecting local neighbourhoods; an autobiography for a youth magazine, issues on gender and inclusion, web page, blog, or zine).
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.2.2:Apply writing skills to specific life situations
indicator_description: Create dialogues between two interlocutors on different themes
examplars:
Compose dialogues of different lengths on given topics by using the following strategies: say the dialogue out loud... keep your dialogue brief and impactful... give each character a unique voice... add world-appropriate slang... be consistent with the characters' voices... remember who they are speaking to... avoid long dialogue paragraphs... cut out greetings
Use appropriate grammatical conventions and structures e.g. capitalisation... use of speech (quotation) marks... comma... question mark where needed... full-stops
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.4.3.1: Research to build and present knowledge
indicator_description: Identify and record information from non-text sources (figures and tables), organise and present it in writing
examplars:
Identify and record: source information (title, author, date etc.)... headings to help you identify the key topics... key points, examples, names, new ideas... triggers to make your notes more memorable - such as mnemonics, colour or drawings... further reading and ideas to follow up later.
Identify and select key ideas.
Organise ideas and make connections: (Write sentences to describe simple graphical data accurately).
Edit/Proofread the writing for sense or meaning, and effect.
Record/represent writing in a flow chart, illustrations and notes in other media including ICT.
Describe simple graphical data in words during presentation.
core_competencies: CC, PL, CI, CP, CG, DL

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.5.1.1: Demonstrate understanding of how various elements of literary genres contribute to meaning
indicator_description: Demonstrate understanding of oral literature (narratives, poetry, drama) and how the different genres contribute to meaning
examplars:
Narrative: Identify and distinguish between the types of oral narratives: folktales, myths and legends. Write simple folktales, myths and legends.
Poetry: Read aloud and distinguish between the types of poetry by function (African poems such as dirges, lullabies, war and praise songs) paying attention to the rhythmic variations. Discuss the cultural and social significance of the poems. Write and recite poems (African poems such as dirges, lullabies, war and praise songs).
Drama: Read and listen to traditional drama (drama according to social function: outdooring, puberty rites, marriages, festivals and enstoolment/enskinment etc.) texts. Distinguish types of traditional drama according to their social functions. Determine cultural/social significance of drama. Act or perform a traditional drama.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.5.1.1: Demonstrate understanding of how various elements of literary genres contribute to meaning
indicator_description: Analyse the elements of written literature (narrative, drama, or poetry)
examplars:
Narrative: Identify and describe the plot, setting, characters. Compare plot, setting and characters between two texts. Compose and read out simple narratives.
Drama: Identify and describe the plot, setting, characters. Compare plot, setting and characters between two texts. Compose and act out a simple drama.
Poetry: Read and distinguish between poems looking at the different types and functions. Discuss and make connections with values in poems. Compose and perform poems illustrating values e.g. love for neighbour, respect for one another and public property etc.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B7.5.1.1: Demonstrate understanding of how various elements of literary genres contribute to meaning
indicator_description: Use basic literary devices in texts (e.g. metaphor, simile, personification, alliteration, assonance, consonance, etc.)
examplars:
Identify the use of basic literary devices in selected genres (poetry, narrative and drama).
Use basic literary devices in texts (e.g. metaphor, simile, personification, alliteration, assonance, consonance, etc).
